name: Cheswick Atomic Division
description_short: Cheswick Atomic Division Federal Credit Union (CADFCU) is a member-owned credit union serving the Harwick, PA area with limited hours and basic financial services.
description_long: Cheswick Atomic Division Federal Credit Union (CADFCU) operates as a federal credit union based in Harwick, Pennsylvania, serving members in the local community. The organization is NCUA-insured and operates on a not-for-profit, member-owned model typical of credit unions. The institution maintains a physical office location at P.O. Box 518, Harwick, PA 15049 and provides phone and email contact options for member inquiries. CADFCU offers core credit union services including share accounts and deposit products, though the website provides limited detail on the full range of offerings. Members can access services during posted office hours: Monday, Wednesday, and Friday from 7:00–8:30 AM and 2:30–4:00 PM. The credit union maintains two phone numbers (724-275-5071 and 724-274-4585) and an email address for member support. The organization recently underwent a core software update and has established error resolution processes to address any related issues. What distinguishes CADFCU is its highly localized focus and extremely limited operating hours, which reflect a small membership base and minimal staffing. The credit union explicitly announces holiday closures (Thanksgiving and Black Friday) and uses basic web communication to notify members of service updates. This grassroots approach suggests CADFCU serves primarily local members with ties to the Cheswick/Harwick area. CADFCU is best suited for members who value local, not-for-profit financial institutions and can accommodate the restricted operating schedule. The minimal online presence and limited service descriptions indicate this is a traditional, small-scale credit union rather than a full-service financial institution. Prospective members should contact the organization directly to understand the complete range of services available, as the website provides little specificity about loan products, rates, or account types.
services: Payday Alternative Loans (typical credit union offering, not explicitly detailed on site)
Member support via phone and email
Core banking software and account management systems
Basic financial services for local community members
pros: NCUA-insured member deposits provide federal protection up to standard limits
Not-for-profit, member-owned structure means profits are returned to members rather than shareholders
Local credit union serving the Harwick, PA community with in-person branch access
Multiple contact methods available: phone (2 numbers) and email for member support
Proactive communication about system updates and error resolution processes
No website bloat or aggressive sales tactics—straightforward, honest presentation
cons: Extremely limited hours (only 3 days per week, 3 hours total) make access difficult for working members
Minimal website content provides little information about services, rates, loan products, or membership requirements
Small institution size may limit product variety, technology capabilities, and customer service resources
No information about online banking, mobile apps, or remote account management options
Vague service offerings make it unclear whether CADFCU offers loans, credit cards, or other products beyond basic share accounts
best_for: Local Harwick/Cheswick area residents with schedule flexibility matching posted hours
Members seeking a traditional, not-for-profit alternative to commercial banks
Individuals prioritizing community banking relationships over digital convenience
Depositors looking for NCUA insurance on savings with local institutional oversight
diagnosis: CADFCU is best for local Harwick, PA residents who can visit during the limited Monday/Wednesday/Friday hours and prefer member-owned, not-for-profit banking. The major caveat is that the website provides almost no information about specific products, rates, or terms—interested parties must contact the credit union directly to understand what services are actually available.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_description: Cheswick Atomic Division — NCUA-insured credit union in Harwick, PA. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
